verdict: RES-5 tail budget CLOSES for I<=1e-3 (ratio<0.04, ~27x margin) and is MARGINAL/estimate-undetermined at the I=2e-3 endpoint (ratio ~1.2). RES-5 localised to the endpoint = SC-SCOPE named-hypothesis boundary. No tier flip (B1 T6 on {H-LAYER}).

claims.name: a0_scales_with_intensity
claims.detail: (a0(2e-3)/a0(1e-3) = 1.90 ~ 2: a0=2 lam' I/rhat is ~proportional to I, so the screened tail C_G a0 halves from the endpoint to I=1e-3)

claims.name: endpoint_marginal_undetermined
claims.detail: (I=2e-3 endpoint: tail=0.0471 vs slack=0.0385, ratio=1.22 ~ 1 -- the screened higher-skeleton tail and the thin slack are the SAME ORDER; the all-orders endpoint selection is estimate-UNDETERMINED (could go either way within estimate uncertainty). RES-5 OPEN at the endpoint)

claims.name: budget_closes_offendpoint_I_1e-3
claims.detail: (I=1e-3: tail=0.0247 << slack=0.677, ratio=0.036 (~27x margin) -- the budget closes decisively; the tail halves AND the slack grows ~18x off the endpoint)

claims.name: budget_closes_offendpoint_I_4e-4
claims.detail: (I=4e-4: tail=0.0102 << slack=0.875, ratio=0.012 -- closes with even larger margin)

claims.name: res5_localised_to_endpoint
claims.detail: (tail/slack improves by 34x from the endpoint to I=1e-3: RES-5 is NOT a global obstruction -- it is localised to the I=2e-3 endpoint, exactly where SC-SCOPE is B1's named second-cumulant hypothesis)
